name: FEMA Individual Assistance
requiredDocs: Photo ID
Social Security number
Proof of occupancy or ownership (deed, lease, or utility bill)
Insurance policy information or denial letter
List of disaster-caused damages and losses
Bank account information for direct deposit
description: Grants for homeowners and renters to cover temporary housing, essential home repairs, and other disaster-related needs not covered by insurance. This is usually the first program to apply to.

name: SBA Disaster Loans
requiredDocs: Photo ID
FEMA registration number
Most recent federal income tax return
Proof of ownership or lease
Insurance settlement or denial documentation
List of damaged property and estimated losses
description: Low-interest, long-term loans for homeowners (up to $500,000 for real estate), renters, and businesses to repair or replace disaster-damaged property. You do not have to accept a loan if approved.

name: NC Disaster Recovery Assistance
agency: NC Office of Recovery & Resiliency (NCORR / ReBuild NC)
requiredDocs: Photo ID for all household members over 18
Proof of homeownership (deed or property tax record)
Proof that the home is your primary residence
Household income documentation for the past 30 days
FEMA award or denial letter
Insurance documentation
description: State-funded rehabilitation, reconstruction, and reimbursement for owner-occupied homes damaged by Hurricane Helene, prioritizing low-to-moderate income households. Fills gaps left by FEMA and insurance.

name: USDA Emergency Farm Loans
requiredDocs: Photo ID
Farm operating records and production history
Proof of ownership or lease of the farm operation
Documentation of crop, livestock, or equipment losses
Most recent federal income tax return
Insurance or crop coverage documentation
description: Low-interest emergency loans to help farmers and ranchers in declared counties recover from production and physical losses, including replacing equipment, livestock, and restoring farmland.

name: Disaster Unemployment Assistance
requiredDocs: Photo ID and Social Security number
Proof of employment or self-employment at time of disaster
Most recent federal income tax return (for self-employed)
Documentation showing work loss was caused by the disaster
Bank account information for payment
description: Weekly benefits for workers and the self-employed who lost their jobs or income as a direct result of the disaster and do not qualify for regular unemployment insurance.

name: NC 211 Resource Line
requiredDocs: No documents required to call or search
description: Free, confidential referral service (dial 2-1-1) connecting survivors to local resources: food, shelter, cleanup, mental health, and other assistance. A good starting point when you are not sure where to turn.

name: Baptists on Mission Disaster Relief
requiredDocs: No application fee or documents required to request help
Description and photos of the damage (helpful but not required)
description: Volunteer-powered mud-out, cleanup, tree removal, temporary roof repair, and rebuilding for homeowners at no cost. Focused on western NC communities recovering from flooding.
